content.full_text:
11137 Clarkston Avenue  
Cupertino, CA 95014  
October 18, 1991

Chief, Technical Assistance Division  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sir or Madam:

This is a request for advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b).

I am a member of the Cupertino Union School District Board of Trustees. I also have a number of business interests, and in 1989 was a partner with Mr. Daren Shu in Cerrito Heights Development Inc., which had been formed to manage a project in Contra Costa County.

In the fall of 1989, the Cupertino Union School District conducted a public auction to sell 53 lots at a former school site in the city of Sunnyvale. Over a hundred people bid on these lots, including Mr. Daren Shu, who put in a high bid for seven of them, paying substantially over the minimum bid. Mr. Shu formed a partnership, the S.V. Inverness Estates Limited Partnership, to purchase and develop the lots. Because S.V. Inverness Estates was awaiting approval from the Secretary of State, Mr. Shu used the Cerrito Heights name in making the bid. I was not and am not a partner in S.V. Inverness Estates, and Cerrito Heights had no financial interest in the transaction. Nonetheless, I abstained from the vote to approve the bids.

Subsequently, a company in which I did have a financial interest contracted with S.V. Inverness Estates to design and build homes on each of the seven lots.

Under these circumstances do I have any reporting obligation or did I have a duty to do anything other than abstain from participating in the vote?

Thank you for your attention to this request.

Sincerely,

Tommy Shue

Dear Friends and Supporters:

This morning a San Jose Mercury News reporter, Benard Bauer, printed a totally inaccurate, misleading and unfounded story charging me with a conflict of interest in my duties as a Trustee for the Cupertino Union School District. To demonstrate my innocence from this allegation, I have submitted the facts to the Fair Political Practices Commission requesting their advice. A copy of the letter is enclosed for your information.

Thank you for your continuing support. Together we can make a difference to shape Cupertino's future and continue the quality of life we are blessed with here in our city. I look forward to seeing you at our VICTORY CELEBRATION on November 5th!

Sincerely,

Tommy Shue
